entries.title: ITR-1 (Sahaj)
entries.body: For resident individuals (not being not-ordinarily-resident) with total income up to ₹50 lakh, income only from: salary/pension, one house property (no brought-forward loss), other sources (excluding lottery/race-horses), and agricultural income up to ₹5,000. Cannot be used if you have capital gains, foreign assets, directorship in a company, unlisted equity shares, or TDS u/s 194N.

entries.title: ITR-2
entries.body: For individuals and HUFs not having income from profits and gains of business or profession. Typical cases: salary + capital gains, multiple house properties, foreign income/assets, directorship, unlisted equity. Salaried filers with equity or mutual-fund capital gains usually fall here.

entries.title: ITR-3
entries.body: For individuals and HUFs having income from profits and gains of business or profession — including F&O traders treating it as business income, and partners in partnership firms. All ITR-2 income heads are also declared here.

entries.title: ITR-4 (Sugam)
entries.body: For resident individuals/HUFs/firms (other than LLP) with presumptive income u/s 44AD (business), 44ADA (profession), or 44AE (goods transport), and total income up to ₹50 lakh. Cannot be used if you have capital gains, more than one house property, or foreign assets.

entries.title: ITR-U (updated return)
entries.body: The updated return form under Section 139(8A). Used to voluntarily declare additional income missed in the original/revised return, within 24 months (extending to 48 months as per Finance Act 2025) from the end of the AY. Cannot be used to claim a refund or reduce tax liability; additional tax of 25% or 50% applies over base tax + interest.
